title: DB Connection Timeout on payment-service due to connection leak in v2.3.1

timeline.event: Alert triggered: DB_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT on payment-service in us-east-1.
Initial triage identified connection pool exhaustion as the likely cause.
HikariCP metrics confirmed active connections exceeded pool size of 50.
Database query revealed unclosed connections originating from PaymentRepository.
Rolled back payment-service to v2.3.0 as a temporary mitigation.
Connection pool size increased from 50 to 100 to handle traffic spikes.
Hotfix v2.3.2 deployed to patch connection leak in PaymentRepository.
Incident resolved: DB connection timeouts ceased and service stabilized.

confirmed_root_cause: Connection leak introduced in v2.3.1 - PaymentRepository failed to close connections on timeout path, exhausting the HikariCP pool under load.
contributing_factors: High traffic volume exacerbated connection pool exhaustion.
Lack of automated connection leak detection in pre-production environments.
Delayed rollback decision due to ongoing investigation of alternative hypotheses.
impact: Payment-service experienced intermittent DB connection timeouts, resulting in failed payment transactions and degraded customer experience. Approximately 15% of payment requests failed during the incident window.
resolution_summary: Rolled back payment-service to v2.3.0, increased connection pool size from 50 to 100, and deployed hotfix v2.3.2 to patch the connection leak in PaymentRepository.

action_items.action: Implement automated connection leak detection in staging and production environments.
Conduct a full code review of PaymentRepository to identify any other potential lifecycle issues.
Add monitoring alerts for abnormal connection pool usage patterns.
Update runbooks to include rollback procedures for connection pool exhaustion scenarios.
Review and optimize database query execution plans for /api/payment and /api/refund endpoints.

lessons_learned: The incident highlighted the importance of automated detection for connection leaks and the need for faster rollback decisions during critical outages. Additionally, pre-production environments must simulate high-traffic scenarios to uncover lifecycle issues before deployment.
prevention_steps: Implement automated connection leak detection tools in staging and production environments.
Enhance pre-production testing to simulate high-traffic scenarios and identify lifecycle issues.
Add monitoring alerts for abnormal connection pool usage patterns.
Update runbooks with detailed rollback procedures for connection pool exhaustion scenarios.
